description: 45-day odd first period on Wikipedia $200k/30yr/6.5% — NEGATIVE-PATH fixture: f = 45/30 = 1.5 violates D-16 (f must be < 1 unit period); engine raises ValueError.
citation: 12 CFR §1026.17(c)(4) + Appendix J §(b)(5)(iii) + Phase 7 D-16 (long odd first period >= 1 unit period rejected)

expected.note: D-26 LOCKED: long odd first period (>= 1 unit period) is a NEGATIVE-path test asserting ValueError; do NOT attempt to compute APR for this fixture. Per Plan 07-03 D-16: helper returns f in [-1, 1); long cases >= 1 unit period rejected (caller should insert an extra t=1 advance to model the long delay). This fixture documents the boundary; sibling test_odd_first_period_too_long_raises asserts the engine raises with the documented message substring.
